title: Pass me not, O gentle Savior
firstLine: Pass me not, O gentle Savior,
publicDomainBasis: Fanny Jane Crosby (1820–1915), William Howard Doane (1832–1915). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Pass me not, O gentle Savior,
Hear my humble cry;
While on others Thou art calling,
Do not pass me by.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
Let me at Thy throne of mercy
Find a sweet relief;
Kneeling there in deep contrition,
Help my unbelief.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
Trusting only in Thy merit,
Would I seek Thy face;
Heal my wounded, broken spirit,
Save me by Thy grace.

sections.label: Chorus
sections.text:
Thou the spring of all my comfort,
More than life to me;
Whom have I on earth beside Thee?
Whom in heaven but Thee?
